revision.comment: Bot: mark 2 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24122753975|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:粟田神社]] {{Otheruses|the shrine in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|ja|東山区 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|the shrine in {{ill|Hachiman, Izu city, Shizuoka|ja|八幡 (伊豆市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman, Izu city, Shizuoka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Kinomiya Jinja (Izushi Hachiman)|the shrine in {{ill|Shirata, Higashiizu, Kamo District, Shizuoka|ja|白田 (東伊豆町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirata, Higashiizu, Kamo District, Shizuoka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Shiritawo Yoriki Jinja|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinto]] | cult_centre = {{ill|Awata Shrine|ja|粟田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata Shrine|qq=}}
[[Yasaka Shrine]]
{{ill|Katano-jinja|ja|片埜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katano-jinja|1=Katano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11569554}}
{{ill|Suga Shrine|en|Suga Shrine|qid=Q11664579|lt=Suga-jinja|1=Suga Shrine}}
{{ill|Hyōsu-jinja|ja|兵主神社 (西脇市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōsu-jinja|1=Hyōsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28684010}} | father = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | mother = [[Kushinadahime]] | children = {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Awata-jinja Shrine, Kyoto.jpg | deity = {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|qid=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi|1=Yashimajinumi}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Awata Jinja (Awata Shrine) | location= Awataguchi Kaji-chō 1, {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 00 | lat_sec = 29.6 | lon_deg =135 | lon_min = 47 | lon_sec = 7.5 | iso = JP-26 | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}|{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}|qq=}} | type = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | related = Kyoto Jūrokusha (Sixteen Shrines of Kyoto), Kyoto Tōken Goshuin Meguri (Kyoto Sword Goshūin Tour) | founded = {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 18 (876) | honden = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | festival= October 15 | ceremonies = Night-walk ritual (before Health and Sports Day), Shinkō-sai on Health and Sports Day, Kinen-sai (Yudate) on February 17, Niiname-sai and Hitaki-sai on November 23 | other_name = Kanjin-in Shingū
Awata Tennōsha
Awata Tennōgū
Awata Hachidaijōji-sha
Kanki-in (Bettō-ji, Shingon-shū) | map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | label_position = left | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|qid=Q1203862|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.008222222222|N|135.78541666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} {{osm box|w|440751886|qq=}} '''Awata Jinja''' (粟田神社, Awata Shrine) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. Under the modern shrine Rank system, it was formerly a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. [[Awata Shrine]] is a shrine of the [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] that worships {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|qid=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi|1=Yashimajinumi}}{{Cite web |title=Awata Taisai |url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/october/awata-taisai-awata-shrine/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=Discover Kyoto |language=en|qq=}} == Enshrined Deities == * Main enshrined deities: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Left side: {{ill|Hachiōji Gongen|lt=Hachiōji Gongen|lt=Hachioji Gongen|ja|八王子権現|lt=Hachidaioji no Mikoto (eight princes, children of Susanoo)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Gongen|qq=}} * Right side: {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime}}, {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|ja|神大市比売命|lt=Kamu Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuo Ichihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売}}, {{ill|Sasura-hime no Mikoto|ja|佐須良比売命|lt=Sasura-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasura-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Also enshrined in the outer hall on the right side are deities from {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|en|Tsukubusuma Shrine|qid=Q11643470|lt=Chikubu Island Shrine|1=Tsukubusuma Shrine}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine|en|Sarutahiko Shrine|qid=Q11572141|lt=Sarutahiko-sha|1=Sarutahiko Shrine}}, Dokai-sha, {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}, {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, Waka sanshin (three poetry gods), {{ill|Tajikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Sutoku Tennō|1=Emperor Sutoku}}. The Hachidaioji no Mikoto are the eight children of Susanoo: {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|qid=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi|1=Yashimajinumi}}, {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Oyayahiko no Kami|ja|大屋彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyayahiko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Oyayatsuhime no Kami|ja|大屋津媛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyayatsuhime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135503402}}, {{ill|Tsumatsuhime no Kami|ja|抓津媛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumatsuhime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135503405}}, {{ill|Suseri-hime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理媛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503408|1=Suseri-hime}}, {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Uka no Mitama no Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}. == History == During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, in the era of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505731|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, in Jōgan 18 (876), the Jingikan and Onmyōryō reported to the emperor that “there will be military disasters on the borders this year, and in autumn epidemics will plague the people.” The emperor issued an edict for offerings to various gods for national safety. {{ill|Fujiwara no Okiyō|ja|藤原興世|lt=Fujiwara no Okiyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Okiyō|1=Fujiwara no Okiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126612}}, as an imperial messenger, prayed devotedly for seven days and seven nights at Kanjin’in Gion-sha (present-day {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}). On the last night, a venerable old man appeared in Okiyō’s dream, identifying himself as {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|lt=Ōnamuchi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and telling him to build a shrine at a pure place northeast of Gion, related to Gozu Tennō (Susanoo), and to enshrine him there. Another theory states that the {{ill|Awata clan|ja|粟田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata clan|1=Awata clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55532751}} who ruled this area since {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}’s era founded the shrine as their ujigami (clan deity). Afterwards, the shrine became the chinju (tutelary) shrine of {{ill|Shōren-in|en|Shōren-in|qid=Q377386|lt=Shōren-in|1=Shōren-in}}. Because it enshrines the same deities as Gion’s Kanjin’in Gion-sha (Susanoo and Ōnamuchi), Awata Jinja was once called Kanjin’in Shingū (Kanjin-in 11 Shrine) and also Awata Tennō-gū, but in the Meiji era it was renamed Awata Jinja and designated a Prefectural Shrine. Located at Awataguchi, one of the {{ill|Seven Entrances of Kyoto|ja|京の七口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Entrances of Kyoto|1=Kyoto's Seven Entrances|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9385072}}, it has long been worshipped as a protector for safe travel. Even now, people heading off on journeys offer ema or buy talismans here.Satō Tsuyoshi “A 11 Departure, Praying for Safety” Asahi Shimbun, March 14, 2013, Morning edition, p.30 In ancient times, swordsmiths lived around Awataguchi. They were also called Sanjō Kokanji since they lived by {{ill|Sanjō Street|en|Sanjō Street|qid=Q11355860|lt=Sanjō Street|1=Sanjō Street}}. From the Heian to Kamakura periods, renowned smiths like {{ill|Sanjō Munechika|ja|三条宗近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjō Munechika|qq=}} and {{ill|Awataguchi Yoshimitsu|ja|粟田口吉光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awataguchi Yoshimitsu|1=Awataguchi Yoshimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604625}} emerged. Although no notable smiths appeared in the Muromachi period, Awataguchi’s fame persisted and even appeared in the Muromachi-era kyōgen play "Awataguchi."Heibonsha, Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 27: “Kyoto-shi no Chimei” p.305 For this reason, the shrine has a Kaji (smith) Jinja dedicated to Munechika, Yoshimitsu, and {{ill|Ame-no-Mahitotsu no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|lt=Tenmahitotsu no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu no Kami|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10016750}}, the ancestor of metalworkers.Kaji Jinja on-site information board == festivals == The shrine’s main festivals—Yowatari Shinji (Night Crossing Ritual) before Health and Sports Day, Shinkō-sai on Health and Sports Day, and its festival on October 15—are collectively known as the Awata Matsuri (Awata festival) or Awata Jinja Taisai or Awataguchi Matsuri. The Awata Matsuri supposedly began in Chōhō 3 (1001), on the old calendar’s September 9, when a divine child at Gion-sha told a kami that a sign would appear northeast of Gion in 7 days, and on September 15 a miraculous light did appear at this shrine’s location. Previously, the festival day was September 15 (old calendar).Miyaji Naokazu & Saeki Ariyoshi (eds.), "Shintō Daijiten," Rinsen Shoten, 1996 (reprint), p.72 The Shinkō-sai features kenboko (spear-like halberds) and mikoshi processions. The Awata Matsuri’s kenboko are said to be the prototypes of Gion Matsuri’s yamaboko floats. Once coinciding with Okazaki Matsuri of {{ill|Okazaki Jinja|ja|岡崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Jinja|1=Okazaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11472971}}, Okazaki Matsuri moved to October 16, a day after Awata Matsuri."Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 26: Kyoto-shi no Chimei," Heibonsha, 1981, p.321 Kenboko are ritual implements in goryō-e. Many Kyoto festivals use them. As of 2012, there were 51 shrines in Kyoto preserving kenboko.Asahi Shimbun, September 17, 2012 Awata Jinja has 18 kenboko dating back to the Muromachi and Edo periods, each 7-8 m long and weighing 40-60 kg.Yomiuri Shimbun, September 26, 2011 In 2011 one was newly made and called the “Crane Halberd.” By 2012, one to six kenboko appeared in the festival. According to documents from Kyōhō 2 (1717), the mikoshi procession route once extended to Nijo-dōri and the Nanzenji Sōmon gate. Due to repair costs after a 1959 typhoon damaged the worship hall’s roof, the mikoshi procession halted in 1960. But it revived in 2000, the 1000th anniversary of Awata Matsuri’s origin.Asahi Shimbun, October 7, 2000 In the 1830s, there were large lanterns reminiscent of nebuta floats, but they died out. In 2008, working with Kyoto University of Art and Design students, about five large lantern floats (Awata Ōtōro), 2 m wide × 2.5 m tall × 3 m deep, were revived.Asahi Shimbun, October 13, 2008 == Precincts == * Honden (main hall, Kyoto City designated tangible cultural property) * Heiden (Kyoto City designated tangible cultural property) * Haiden (Kyoto City designated tangible cultural property) - rebuilt in Genroku 16 (1703) * Kagura-den * Shamusho (Office) == Subsidiary Shrines == === Sessha (Associated Shrines) === * Kitamuki Inari Jinja * Shusse Ebisu Jinja - Originally enshrined in Ebi Valley of Keage in Higashiyama. After Genpei resurgent prayed there before going down to Mutsu. Later moved to near Konrō-ji north of here and finally in the Meiji era to current location. The Ebisugami image, said to be carved by {{ill|Saichō|en|Saichō|qid=Q449943|lt=Saichō|1=Saichō}}, is the oldest wooden Ebisu image in Japan. * Shōten-sha - Has an image carved by Tangkai Hōzan risshi, a former bettō of the shrine. He later founded {{ill|Hōzan-ji|en|Hōzan-ji|qid=Q762525|lt=Hōzan-ji|1=Hōzan-ji}} in Yamato’s Ikoma mountains. === Massha (Minor Shrines) === * Ōmikami-gū - from the residence of Torii Kōji, a monk official of Shōren-in, moved here in Meiji era * Kaji Jinja - Deities: {{ill|Sanjō Munechika|ja|三条宗近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjō Munechika|qq=}}, {{ill|Awataguchi Yoshimitsu|ja|粟田口吉光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awataguchi Yoshimitsu|1=Awataguchi Yoshimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604625}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Mahitotsu no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Located halfway up Awata Jinja approach on the east side.[http://awatajinja.jp/precincts/ Awata Jinja website precincts] Known for blades, smithing, victory and good fortune. * Taga-sha * Asahi Tenmangū * Kichibee Jinja * Tarōbee Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Honden * Heiden * Haiden == Notes == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Gakuchōzan Rion-ji|ja|華頂山良恩寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuchōzan Rion-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Dōinji, Nenbutsudō, Kaminodō; sōdō of Awataguchi Village) * {{ill|Shōren-in|en|Shōren-in|qid=Q377386|lt=Shōren-in|1=Shōren-in}} * {{ill|Sonshō-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊勝院 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonshō-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Aizuchi Inari Jinja|ja|合槌稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuchi Inari Jinja|1=Aizuchi Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48763373}} * {{ill|Kachōzan Chion-in|ja|華頂山知恩院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kachōzan Chion-in|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Awata-jinja|qq=}} * [http://www.awatajinja.jp Awata Jinja] (Official site) * {{Twitter|qq=}} * {{Facebook|qq=}} * {{Instagram|qq=}} {{Gion cult|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto|qq=}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|粟田神社|version=99045810|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories||qq=}} {{everybodywiki link|Awata Shrine}} [[ceb:Awata Shrine]] [[ja:粟田神社]] {{Hidden category}} {{Commons category|Awata-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11604632}} [[Category:京都市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:東山区の建築物]] [[Category:東山区の歴史]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:粟田口]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
